name: Chinese Fried Pecans
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: This dish must be started a full 24 hours before you plan to serve it.
1. Place pecans in a large pot or heat-proof bowl.
2. Cover with boiling water and let them soak for 3 minutes.
3. Drain the pecans and spread them in a single layer on a large platter or non-rusting cookie sheet.
4. Cover with sugar and stir until pecans are completely coated with the sugar (the sugar will dissolve slightly). Add more water if needed so the sugar sticks to the nuts.
5. Transfer the sugared nuts to a dry platter and set aside to air-dry overnight (24 hours).
6. When ready to serve, heat oil in a wok or large pot until it almost reaches the smoking point.
7. Fry one quarter of the pecans at a time, being careful not to let them burn. The sugar will melt during frying.
8. Carefully remove the cooked nuts from the hot oil using a strainer and place them on a heatproof glass platter.
9. Continue frying each quarter of the nuts individually until all are hot enough for the sugar to melt completely.
10. Serve immediately.
